---
title: "Projects"
---

Below are selected graduate-level projects reflecting my work at the intersection of global health, data science, and policy analysis. Each project is written for a general audience while grounded in rigorous methodology.

---

```{=html}
<div class="project-card">
  <h3>Climate Adaptation &amp; Maternal Health: A Cost-Effectiveness Analysis</h3>
  <div class="project-tags">
    <span class="tag">Climate &amp; Health</span>
    <span class="tag">Cost-Effectiveness Analysis</span>
    <span class="tag">Maternal Health</span>
    <span class="tag">R</span>
    <span class="tag">Global Health</span>
  </div>
  <p>Postpartum hemorrhage (PPH)&mdash;excessive bleeding after childbirth&mdash;is a leading cause of maternal death in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). Rising global temperatures make this worse: heat degrades the standard drug used to prevent PPH (oxytocin), reducing its effectiveness in hot climates. This project builds a cost-effectiveness model comparing standard oxytocin against heat-stable carbetocin, a newer alternative that remains effective without refrigeration.</p>
  <p>Using climate projections from CMIP6 and population data across 2020&ndash;2059, the model estimates how many maternal deaths could be prevented and at what cost across different world regions, providing decision-makers with evidence to guide maternal health procurement in a warming world.</p>
  <a class="btn-github" href="https://github.com/TanmayeeKodali/heat-stable-carbetocin" target="_blank">
    <i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub
  </a>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
  <h3>Pulmonary Embolism Incidence Patterns: PE-EHR+ Study</h3>
  <div class="project-tags">
    <span class="tag">Health Informatics</span>
    <span class="tag">Electronic Health Records</span>
    <span class="tag">Python</span>
    <span class="tag">Epidemiology</span>
  </div>
  <p>Pulmonary embolism (PE)&mdash;a blood clot in the lungs&mdash;is a life-threatening but often preventable condition. Despite its severity, patterns of PE diagnosis and care complexity are not well-characterized across diverse patient populations. This project uses electronic health record (EHR) data from the PE-EHR+ dataset to analyze how PE is diagnosed, managed, and documented across hospitals and patient groups.</p>
  <p>Working in Python, the analysis examines trends in PE incidence, differences across demographic groups, and the complexity of clinical care pathways&mdash;surfacing insights that can improve hospital protocols and reduce preventable mortality.</p>
  <a class="btn-github" href="https://github.com/TanmayeeKodali/pe-ehr-study" target="_blank">
    <i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub
  </a>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
  <h3>SARS-CoV-2 Variant Dominance Analysis (2022&ndash;2024)</h3>
  <div class="project-tags">
    <span class="tag">Epidemiology</span>
    <span class="tag">Infectious Disease</span>
    <span class="tag">R</span>
    <span class="tag">Data Visualization</span>
    <span class="tag">Reproducible Research</span>
  </div>
  <p>As the COVID-19 pandemic evolved, successive waves were driven by different SARS-CoV-2 variants&mdash;each with distinct transmission rates and clinical severity. Understanding which variants dominated, and when, is essential for evaluating public health responses and preparing for future outbreaks.</p>
  <p>This project analyzes U.S. surveillance data from December 2022 through October 2024, tracking the emergence and dominance of variants that achieved greater than 5% national prevalence. Using R with fully reproducible, version-controlled code, the analysis produces clear visualizations to inform vaccine strategy and outbreak preparedness planning.</p>
  <a class="btn-github" href="https://github.com/TanmayeeKodali/finalproject" target="_blank">
    <i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub
  </a>
</div>

<p style="font-size: 0.9rem; color: #5a6a70; margin-top: 1rem;">
  <i class="fab fa-github"></i> Explore all repositories on my <a href="https://github.com/TanmayeeKodali?tab=repositories" target="_blank">GitHub profile</a>.
</p>
```
